Cereus grandicornis, also known as Selenicereus grandiflorus, is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. 

Research on Cereus grandicornis is limited, but it may offer some health benefits due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. 

Bioactive constituents of Cereus grandicornis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. 

Cereus grandicornis is not commonly used in herbal preparations, but it can be found in some traditional and folk medicine, often used in its raw or dried form, or as an extract.

Note: More research is needed on Cereus grandicornis to confirm its uses and properties.
taxonomy: Cactus grandicornis is classified within the family Cactaceae, specifically in the genus Pachycereus. It is a species of cactus that is native to the Americas. Within the plant kingdom, Cactus grandicornis is classified as a succulent, a type of plant that has adapted to store water in its leaves or stems. The classification of Cactus grandicornis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Caryophyllales, family Cactaceae, genus Pachycereus, and species Pachycereus grandicornis.
